description_long: This sculpture depicts a seated figure, likely inspired by ancient Egyptian statuary, carved from a pale stone material. The figure wears a traditional nemes headdress with distinct horizontal striations and a false beard, both characteristic of pharaonic iconography. The arms are positioned with one resting on the lap and the other bent across the chest, and the facial features are stylized with almond-shaped eyes and a serene expression. The figure is seated on a blocky, high-backed throne with simple, rectangular paneling on the sides and back. The surface of the sculpture is matte and slightly rough, with subtle variations in color from off-white to light beige and faint grayish shadows in recessed areas. The overall form is monolithic and solid, with no visible joints or separable parts.
description: A pale stone sculpture of a seated figure in ancient Egyptian style, featuring a nemes headdress and blocky throne. The surface is matte, slightly rough, and monolithic.
description_view_0: Front view shows the full face, headdress, and both arms, with the figure seated upright on a rectangular throne.
description_view_1: Left side view highlights the profile of the face, the arm resting on the lap, and the solid, high-backed throne.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the back of the headdress and the plain, rectangular back of the throne with minimal detail.
description_view_3: Right side view mirrors the left, showing the other profile and the blocky structure of the throne.
attribute_subject_matter: seated human figure in ancient Egyptian style
attribute_location_context: No visible environmental context; background is isolated and white.
